title: Get details of potential audiences
content:
Use this endpoint to get the details of your potential audiences. >**Note**
This endpoint uses the POST method. ## Request **Endpoint** https://business-api.tiktok.com/open_api/v1.3/audience/insight/info/ **Method** POST **Header** ``` xtable |Field|Data Type|Description| |---|---|---| |Access-Token {Required}|string|Authorized access token. For details, see [Authentication](https://ads.tiktok.com/marketing_api/docs?id=1738373164380162).| |Content-Type {Required}|string|Request message type.
Allowed value: `"application/json"`.| ``` **Parameters** ```xtable |Field|Data Type|Description| |---|---|---| |advertiser_id {Required}|string| Advertiser ID.| |custom_audience_id {+Conditional}|string|You need pass in either `locations`or `custom_audience_id` in the request.
Custom audience ID.

Note:

  • When `custom_audience_id` is specified, either `selected_audience` or `locations` cannot be used. Otherwise, an error will occur.
  • The audience ID you input must belong to your specified advertisers.
| |locations {+Conditional}|Object[]| You need pass in either `locations`or `custom_audience_id` in the request.
Locations.
Max size : 5

Note: When `locations` is specified, `custom_audience_id` cannot be used. Otherwise, an error will occur. | #|country_code {Required}|string| Country code. See [Appendix-Location IDs](https://business-api.tiktok.com/portal/docs?id=1739311040498689) for details.| #|location_ids|string[]|Location IDs.
By default, all location IDs under the specified country code are selected. The location IDs you input must fall within the range of the specified country code. | |dimensions{Required}|string[]|Use this field to control which API response dimensions are returned.
Enum values: `age`, `gender`, `country`, `top_regions`, `operating_system`, `operating_system_version`, `device_price`, `top_interests`, `bottom_interests`, `engagement`, `ad_interest_categories`, and `top_hashtags`.

Note: Currently, you can input only one dimension for each API call. | |selected_audience|Object|The audience you want to filter for.| #|top_regions_country_code {+Conditional}|string|Required if you have input `top_regions` in the `dimensions` field and specified `locations`.
When the dimension is `top_regions`, the corresponding return fields will contain the sub-regions within the country you specified in `locations`.| #|languages|string[]|Codes of the languages that you want to target.
For the list of language codes supported, see [Enumeration - Language Code](https://business-api.tiktok.com/portal/docs?id=1737174886619138). You can also get language codes via [/tool/language/](https://ads.tiktok.com/marketing_api/docs?id=1737188554152962).| #|age_groups|string[]|Age groups you want to target.
For enum values, see [Enumeration - Age Group](https://business-api.tiktok.com/portal/docs?id=1737174886619138#item-link-Age%20Group).| #|gender|string[]|Genders that you want to target.
Enum values: `GENDER_FEMALE`, `GENDER_MALE`. If you don't pass anything, it means that you want to target both males and females. | #|device_price_ranges|integer[]|Targeting device price range.
10000 means 1000+. The numbers must be in multiples of 50.| #|min_ios_version|string[]|Minimum iOS version.
For enum values, see [Enumeration - Minimum iOS Version](https://business-api.tiktok.com/portal/docs?id=1737174886619138).| #|min_android_version|string[]|Minimum Android version.
For enum values, see [Enumeration - Minimum Android Version](https://ads.tiktok.com/marketing_api/docs?id=1737174886619138).| #|operating_systems{+Conditional}|string[]|Required if you've specified either `min_ios_version` or `min_android_version`.
Device operating systems that you want to target.
Enum values: `ANDROID`, `IOS`. Only one value is allowed. If you don't pass anything, it means that you want to target both Android and iOS systems.| #|interest_category_ids|string[]|Interest classification.
Use [/tool/interest_category/](https://ads.tiktok.com/marketing_api/docs?id=1737174348712961) to get a list of available interest categories. Use [/tool/target_recommend_tags/](https://ads.tiktok.com/marketing_api/docs?id=1736275204260866) to get a list of recommended interest categories.| #|video_action_category_ids|string[]|IDs of the video action categories.
Use [/tool/action_category/](https://ads.tiktok.com/marketing_api/docs?id=1737166752522241) to get a list of available video interaction categories (`action_scene` = `VIDEO_RELATED` in the response). Use [/tool/targeting_category/recommend/](https://ads.tiktok.com/marketing_api/docs?id=1736275204260866) to get a list of recommended video interaction categories (`scene` = `VIDEO_RELATED` in the response).| #|creator_action_category_ids|string[]|IDs of the creator action categories.
Use [/tool/action_category/](https://ads.tiktok.com/marketing_api/docs?id=1737166752522241) to get a list of available creator interaction categories (`action_scene` = `CREATOR_RELATED` in the response). Use [/tool/targeting_category/recommend/](https://ads.tiktok.com/marketing_api/docs?id=1736275204260866) to get a list of recommended creator interaction categories (`scene` = `CREATOR_RELATED` in the response).| #|hashtag_action_category_ids|string[]|Hashtag IDs.
Use [/tool/hashtag/recommend/](https://business-api.tiktok.com/portal/docs?id=1736271339521025) to get a list of recommended hashtag IDs, or use [/tool/hashtag/get/](https://business-api.tiktok.com/portal/docs?id=1736280889167874) to get hashtags by IDs. | ``` ​ ### Example 1. When you specify `locations` and `selected_audience` ``` xcodeblock (code curl http) curl --location --request POST 'https://business-api.tiktok.com/open_api/v1.3/audience/insight/info/' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --header 'Access-Token: {{Access-Token}}' \ --data '{ "advertiser_id": "{{advertiser_id}}", "dimensions": [ "age" ], "locations": [ { "country_code": "US", "location_ids": [] }, { "country_code": "AU", "location_ids": [ "{{location_ids}}" ] } ], "selected_audience": { "gender": [ "GENDER_MALE" ], "age_groups": [ "AGE_25_34", "AGE_35_44", "AGE_45_54", "AGE_55_100" ], "languages": [ "fr", "en" ], "operating_systems": [ "IOS" ], "device_price_ranges": [ 100, 950 ], "interest_category_ids": [ "{{interest_category_ids}}", "{{interest_category_ids}}" ], "video_action_category_ids": [ "{{video_action_category_ids}}", "{{video_action_category_ids}}" ], "creator_action_category_ids": [ "{{creator_action_category_ids}}", "{{creator_action_category_ids}}" ], "hashtag_action_category_ids": [ "{{hashtag_action_category_ids}}" ] } }' (/code) ``` 2. When you specify `custom_audience_id` ``` xcodeblock (code curl http) curl --location 'https://business-api.tiktok.com/open_api/v1.3/audience/insight/info/' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --header 'Access-Token: {{Access-Token}}' \ --data '{ "advertiser_id": "{{advertiser_id}}", "dimensions": [ "age" ], "custom_audience_id": "{{custom_audience_id}}" }' (/code) ``` ## Response ``` xtable |Field|Data Type|Description| |---|---|---| |code |number|Return code, see [Appendix-Return Codes](https://ads.tiktok.com/marketing_api/docs?id=1737172488964097).| |message |string|Return message, see [Appendix-Return Codes](https://ads.tiktok.com/marketing_api/docs?id=1737172488964097).| |request_id |string|The log id of a request, which uniquely identifies the request.| |data |object|Returned Data. | #|age|object|Age. Returned if you have input `age` in `dimensions` in your request.
Within `all` and `selected` objects listed below, there are two fields that convey specific information. See the code example below for details.

  • `label`: Age group label. It is of string data type.
  • `rate`: Percentage of the label. It is of the number data type.
      • | ##|all|object[]|Age data of all audience, that is, the audience for the selected locations.
      In certain scenarios, `all` will be returned as null. These include instances when:
      • When your audience size is less than 1,000.
      • When you specify `custom_audience_id` in your request
      | ##|selected|object[]|Age data of the audience you filtered for.| #|gender|object|Gender. Returned if you have input `gender` in `dimensions` in your request.
      Within `all` and `selected` objects listed below, there are two fields that convey specific information.
      • `label`: Gender label. It is of string data type.
      • `rate`: Percentage of the label. It is of the number data type.
          • | ##|all|object[]|Gender data of all audience, that is, the audience for the selected locations.
          In certain scenarios, `all` will be returned as null. These include instances when:
          • When your audience size is less than 1,000.
          • When you specify `custom_audience_id` in your request.
          | ##|selected|object[]|Gender data of the audience you filtered for.| #|country|object|Country. Returned if you have input `country` in `dimensions` in your request.
          Within `all` and `selected` objects listed below, there are two fields that convey specific information.
          • `label`: Country name. It is of string data type.
          • `rate`: Percentage of the country. It is of the number data type.
              • | ##|all|object[]|Country data of all audience, that is, the audience for the selected locations.
              In certain scenarios, `all` will be returned as null. These include instances when:
              • When your audience size is less than 1,000.
              • When you specify `custom_audience_id` in your request.
              | ##|selected|object[]|Country data of the audience you filtered for.| #|top_regions|object|Country. Returned if you have input `top_regions` in `dimensions` in your request.
              Within `all` and `selected` objects listed below, there are several fields that convey specific information.
              • `label`: Region name. It is of string data type.
              • `rate`: Percentage of the region. It is of the number data type.
              • `region_id`: Region ID. It is of string data type.
              • `country_code`: Country code. It is of string data type.
                  • | ##|all|object[]|Region data of all audience, that is, the audience for the selected locations.
                  In certain scenarios, `all` will be returned as null. These include instances when:
                  • When your audience size is less than 1,000.
                  • When you specify `custom_audience_id` in your request.
                  | ##|selected|object[]|Region data of the audience you filtered for.| #|operating_system|object|Operating system. Returned if you have input `operating_system` in `dimensions` in your request.
                  Within `all` and `selected` objects listed below, there are several fields that convey specific information.
                  • `label`: This field is used to denote the operating system. It is of string data type.
                  • `rate`: This field is utilized to denote the percentage of the operating system. It is of the number data type.
                      • | ##|all|object[]|Operating system data of all audience, that is, the audience for the selected locations.
                      In certain scenarios, `all` will be returned as null. These include instances when:
                      • When your audience size is less than 1,000.
                      • When you specify `custom_audience_id` in your request .
                      | ##|selected|object[]|Operating system data of the audience you filtered for.| #|operating_system_version|object|Operating system version. Returned if you have input `operating_system_version` in `dimensions` in your request.
                      Within `all` and `selected` objects listed below, there are several fields that convey specific information.
                      • `label`: Operating system version. It is of string data type.
                      • `rate`: Percentage of the operating system version. It is of the number data type.
                          • | ##|all|object[]|Operating system version data of all audience, that is, the audience for the selected locations.
                          In certain scenarios, `all` will be returned as null. These include instances when:
                          • When your audience size is less than 1,000.
                          • When you specify `custom_audience_id` in your request.
                          | ##|selected|object[]|Operating system version data of the audience you filtered for.| #|device_price|object|Device price. Returned if you have input `device_price` in `dimensions` in your request.
                          Within `all` and `selected` objects listed below, there are several fields that convey specific information.
                          • `label`: Device price. It is of string data type.
                          • `rate`: Percentage of the device price. It is of the number data type.
                              • | ##|all|object[]|Device price data of all audience, that is, the audience for the selected locations.
                              In certain scenarios, `all` will be returned as null. These include instances when:
                              • When your audience size is less than 1,000.
                              • When you specify `custom_audience_id` in your request .
                              | ##|selected|object[]|Device price data of the audience you filtered for.| #|top_interests|object|Top interests. Returned if you have input `top_interests` in `dimensions` in your request.
                              Within `all` and `selected` objects listed below, there are several fields that convey specific information.
                              • `label`: Interest category label. It is of string data type.
                              • `rate`: Percentage of the interest category label. It is of the number data type.
                              • `ad_interest_category`: This field is used to denote the first-level interest category. It is of string data type.
                                  • | ##|all|object[]|Interest category data of all audience, that is, the audience for the selected locations.
                                  In certain scenarios, `all` will be returned as null. These include instances when:
                                  • When your audience size is less than 1,000.
                                  • When you specify `custom_audience_id` in your request.
                                  | ##|selected|object[]|Interest category data of the audience you filtered for.| #|bottom_interests|object|Bottom interests. Returned if you have input `bottom_interests` in `dimensions` in your request.
                                  Within `all` and `selected` objects listed below, there are several fields that convey specific information.
                                  • `label`: Interest category label. It is of string data type.
                                  • `rate`: Percentage of the interest category label. It is of the number data type.
                                  • `ad_interest_category`: This field is used to denote the first-level interest category. It is of string data type.
                                      • | ##|all|object[]|Interest category data of all audience, that is, the audience for the selected locations.
                                      In certain scenarios, `all` will be returned as null. These include instances when:
                                      • When your audience size is less than 1,000.
                                      • When you specify `custom_audience_id` in your request.
                                      | ##|selected|object[]|Interest category data of the audience you filtered for.| #|ad_interest_categories|object|Interest categories. Returned if you have input `ad_interest_categories` in `dimensions` in your request.
                                      Within `all` and `selected` objects listed below, there are several fields that convey specific information.
                                      • `label`: Interest category label. It is of string data type.
                                      • `rate`: Percentage of the interest category label. It is of the number data type.
                                      • `type`: Level of the interest category. It is of string data type. Example: "first".
                                          • | ##|all|object[]|Interest category data of all audience, that is, the audience for the selected locations.
                                          In certain scenarios, `all` will be returned as null. These include instances when:
                                          • When your audience size is less than 1,000.
                                          • When you specify `custom_audience_id` in your request .
                                          | ##|selected|object[]|Interest category data of the audience you filtered for.| #|top_hashtags|object|Top hashtags. Returned if you have input `top_hashtags` in `dimensions` in your request.| ##|hashtag_id|string|Hashtag ID.| ##|hashtag_name|string|Hashtag name.| ##|count|number|The number of times the hashtag is used. | #|engagement|object|Engagement metrics. Returned if you have input `engagement` in `dimensions` in your request.
                                          Within `all` and `selected` objects listed below, there are several fields that convey specific information.
                                          • `daily_sessions`: Average number of app opens per user per day. It is of the number data type.
                                          • `daily_session_duration_in_minutes`: Average number of minutes used per user per day. It is of the number data type.
                                          • `daily_video_views`: Average number of video views per user per day. It is of the number data type.
                                          • `daily_comments`: Average number of comments per user per day. It is of the number data type.
                                          • `daily_likes`: Average number of likes per user per day. It is of the number data type.
                                          • `daily_shares`: Average number of shares per user per day. It is of the number data type.
                                          • `total_video_uploads`: Average uploads of selected/all users per day. It is of the number data type.
                                          | ##|all|object[]|Engagement data of all audience, that is, the audience for the selected locations.
                                          In certain scenarios, `all` will be returned as null. These include instances when:
                                          • When your audience size is less than 1,000.
                                          • When you specify `custom_audience_id` in your request.
                                          | ##|selected|object[]|Engagement data of the audience you filtered for.| ``` ### Example 1. When you have specified `locations` and `selected_audience` in your request, the response will be as follows: ``` xcodeblock (code Success-Response http) HTTPS/1.1 200 OK { "code": 0, "message": "OK", "request_id": "{{request_id}}", "data": { "age": { "all": [ { "label": "18-24", "rate": 0.2258 }, { "label": "25-34", "rate": 0.3304 }, { "label": "35-44", "rate": 0.201 }, { "label": "45-54", "rate": 0.1327 }, { "label": "≥55", "rate": 0.1102 } ], "selected": [ { "label": "25-34", "rate": 0.5264 }, { "label": "35-44", "rate": 0.2073 }, { "label": "45-54", "rate": 0.136 }, { "label": "≥55", "rate": 0.1302 } ] } } } (/code); ``` 2. When you have specified `custom_audience_id` in your request, the response will be as follows: ``` xcodeblock (code Success-Response http) HTTPS/1.1 200 OK { "code": 0, "message": "OK", "request_id": "{{request_id}}", "data": { "age": { "all": [], "selected": [ { "label": "18-24", "rate": 0.3896 }, { "label": "25-34", "rate": 0.3775 }, { "label": "35-44", "rate": 0.1485 }, { "label": "45-54", "rate": 0.0485 }, { "label": "≥55", "rate": 0.0359 } ] } } } (/code) ```
